text: The Palo Alto County Board of Supervisors met in regular session. Members
present: Linus Solberg, Chair; Roger Faulstick, Craig Merrill, Keith Wirtz. Absent: Ron
Graettinger. Present: Mark Zaccone, Invenergy; Ron Smith, Joe Schany,
Unless otherwise indicated, all of the motions offered at this meeting were carried
with the following vote: Ayes: Faulstick, Merrill Wirtz, Solberg. Nays: None. Absent:
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Wirtz to approve the agenda for May 25th, 2021. Motion
Motion by Wirtz, 2nd by Merrill to approve the minutes for May 18th, 2021.
Motion by Wirtz, 2nd by Merrill to approve Molly Maguires Class C Liquor License,
Outdoor Service & Sunday Sales. Motion Carried.
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Faulstick to set the completion hearing date for Jt. 63
Branch Main Tile for June 15th, 2021, at 1:30 p.m. at the Election Center. Motion
Sarah Strohman, Community Health Director, met with the Board by conference
call to give an update on COVID19 and vaccine. Palo Alto County has 1 case of COVID.
Vaccine is available at clinics and pharmacy.
Mark Zaccone was present for public comment.
Walter Davis-Oeth, County Engineer, met with the Board for approval of utility
permits, update on roads, and approval of 17th Street contract.
Motion by Faulstick, 2nd by Merrill to approve the contract with Blacktop Services
for the 17th Street overlay project for $151,704. This is a 50/50 cost share project with
the City of Emmetsburg. Motion Carried.
Motion by Merrill, seconded by Wirtz to approve a utility permit for Iowa Lakes
Electric in Section 14 of Walnut Township. Motion Carried.
Motion by Faulstick, seconded by Wirtz to approve an amended utility permit for
Iowa Lakes Regional Water in Section 19 of Walnut Township. Motion Carried.
Motion by Merrill, seconded by Wirtz to approve an amended utility permit for
Iowa Lakes Regional Water in Section 14, 23, 24 of Lost Island Township. Motion
Jamey Whitney & Tomas Thayer, Upper Des Moines Opportunity Inc., met with
the Board for the annual update.
John Hansen, Midwest Construction Consultants, met with the Board by
conference call to discuss the bids for the geotechnical soil evaluation.
Motion by Faulstick, 2nd by Wirtz to reconsider the motion from last week
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Faulstick to cancel contract with Certified Testing
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Faulstick to award contract for geotechnical testing to
Motion by Wirtz, 2nd by Merrill to appoint Bill Brown to replace Allen Stangl as a
commissioner on Drainage District No. 84 & Jt 22 Common outlet assessment. Motion
Collin Klingbeil, Bolton & Menk, met with the Board. Collin filed the Drainage
District No. 84 reclassification report and the common outlet report on Jt. Drainage
Motion by Faulstick, 2nd by Wirtz to set the date for common outlet hearing for Jt
22 Main Open for June 29th, 2021, at 9:00 a.m. by conference call. Motion Carried.
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Faulstick to set the date for Drainage District 84
reclassification hearing for June 29th, 2021, at 10:00 a.m. at the Election Center. Motion
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Wirtz to set the date for Drainage District No. 28
continued reclassification hearing for June 22nd, 2021, at 10 a.m. at the Election Center.
Motion was made by Faulstick and seconded by Merrill to re-levy the following
Drainage Districts a percent of the original assessment to pay for outstanding warrants
Drainage District Original Assessment Dollars of Levy
Motion by Merrill, 2nd by Wirtz to introduce and approve Resolution 5-25-2021:
WHEREAS, Section 331.434 (6) 2013 Code of Iowa, authorizes the Board of
Supervisors to transfer appropriated monies within a service area, without amending the
WHEREAS, such a transfer is necessary to correct current budget expenditures
NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Palo Alto County Board of
Supervisors to authorize the appropriation transfers within the same service area as

decreasing the appropriations in the dollar amounts and Department 61 in the General

SECTION 2: The total sum of $_16,015_______ as detailed above is ordered transferred
to the Local Health budget, increasing the appropriations in the dollar amounts and
Department 23, General Basic Fund, Service Area 3 as follows:

the vote thereon being as follows:
